macro_events.event_summary: Indian PM Modi warns that the Iran war poses severe risks to India and publicly urges citizens to cut fuel consumption, gold purchases, and foreign travel — signaling an oil/FX shock and demand destruction from the world's largest gold importer and a top-3 oil importer.
primary_drivers: Oil supply shock premium from Iran/Middle East conflict raising Brent prices
Demand destruction in gold from India (≈25% of global gold demand) being officially discouraged
India macro stress: weaker INR, current account pressure, reduced outbound tourism/travel

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with significant upstream leverage to Brent; Middle East conflict premium directly lifts realized crude prices and refining crack spreads.
Same upstream Brent leverage as XOM; Chevron's Permian and international production benefit directly from sustained war risk premium in oil.
High-beta US oil producer with the most torque to crude price moves among large caps; Buffett-backed name typically rallies hardest on Middle East supply shocks.
Cheniere benefits as Middle East conflict raises global LNG spot prices and reinforces structural demand for US LNG as Strait of Hormuz risk grows; Asian buyers diversify away from Gulf supply.
Scorpio Tankers and product tanker rates spike on Middle East disruption as longer-haul routings (avoiding Hormuz/Red Sea) increase ton-miles; direct beneficiary of war-driven shipping dislocation.
Diamondback is a pure-play Permian operator with near-100% oil price sensitivity and no refining offset; war premium flows straight to cash flow.

losers.rationale: Wipro ADR — same India-listed risk-off dynamic; Modi's unusually direct warning signals government concern about macro stability, which derates Indian equities broadly.

Barrick Gold — India is ~25% of global gold demand; Modi explicitly urging citizens to cut gold purchases is direct demand destruction for the largest physical gold market, pressuring gold price and producers.
Sibanye-Stillwater — high-beta precious metals miner; if Indian wedding/festival gold demand is officially suppressed, gold tape weakens and leveraged miners underperform disproportionately.
Delta and US carriers with India routes lose volume from Modi's explicit call to cut foreign travel; jet fuel cost spike from same oil shock compounds the margin hit.

ICICI Bank ADR — Indian bank directly exposed to domestic macro: current account deterioration from oil import bill, INR weakness, and slower consumer credit as fuel/gold/travel spending is curtailed.
MakeMyTrip — Indian online travel platform directly named in spirit by Modi's call to cut foreign travel; outbound bookings are the highest-margin segment.

headlines: Modi says Iran war poses severe risks to India, urges cuts in fuel use and gold purchases

macro_events.event_summary: Iran peace talks have stalled, causing mixed European markets and a slide in defense stocks (suggesting prior war-premium unwinding or shift in risk assessment).
primary_drivers: Geopolitical risk repricing — defense stocks giving back gains as immediate escalation premium fades despite stalled talks
Oil supply risk persists (Iran/Strait of Hormuz exposure) keeping crude bid
Safe-haven flow ambiguity — stalled talks ≠ imminent conflict, so defense rally pauses

beneficiaries.rationale: Stalled Iran talks keep sanctions framework intact and preserve Iran oil supply uncertainty, sustaining the geopolitical premium in Brent. XOM has highest US major leverage to crude price via upstream segment.
Pure-play US oil producer with high beta to crude. Stalled talks mean no near-term Iranian barrel relief, supportive of WTI.
E&P with direct exposure to oil-price beta; benefits from continued Iran-related supply risk premium absent a diplomatic breakthrough.

losers.rationale: Lockheed has been a key beneficiary of the Middle East war premium (missiles, PAC-3, JASSM). European defense peers sliding signals global rotation out of defense names on perception that no near-term escalation occurs despite stalled talks.

Raytheon's missile defense and Patriot demand has been bid on Iran/Israel risk. Peer correlation with sliding European defense (Rheinmetall, BAE) typically pulls RTX lower intraday on the same narrative.
Defense prime with similar sentiment-driven beta to Middle East headlines; rotates with LMT/RTX on war-premium unwind days.
General Dynamics moves with the defense-prime basket on geopolitical headline flow; peer correlation with European defense slide.
L3Harris tactical comms/EW exposure correlates with defense basket on Middle East headlines; likely caught in the same rotation as European peers slide.

headlines: European markets mixed as Iran's peace talks stall, defense stocks slide
